name: Rimi e-veikals
description: Rimi’s online grocery store in the Baltics offering home delivery, click-and-collect and Drive pickup for Latvian consumers.

details.targetMarket: Latvian consumers and households wanting online grocery delivery or fast pickup; also small businesses via Rimi to Business

items: Home delivery with same-day windows; standard deliveries and ‘fast’ 90-minute or 58-minute options
Rimi Drive (drive‑through pickup) and in-store pickup points (29-minute fast pickup in selected locations)
Delivery subscription (paid) which waives delivery fees for a period (60-day option)
Free standard delivery for orders above threshold (49.98 EUR Latvia; 59.99 EUR Estonia)
Standard delivery fees vary roughly €1.99–€3.99; fast delivery with partners (Wolt) or own service costs more (€3.99–€7.99)
Large grocery assortment including fresh, frozen and non-food items; cold-chain handled for frozen products
Ability to change or add to orders until picking/fulfilment begins
Integration with Mans Rimi loyalty (personal offers) and mobile app features for shopping lists and offers

missingFeatures: Limited geographic coverage beyond major cities — no consistent nationwide same‑day delivery, leaving room for services covering suburban/rural zones with predictable schedules
No public API or partner/developer platform for B2B integration (inventory, order routing, or marketplace) — opportunity to offer an integrations-first grocery commerce layer
Inventory transparency is limited (customers often only learn about out-of-stock items after picking), so better real‑time stock & replacement preferences would reduce friction
No advanced personalization / meal-planning flow tied to promotions — Rimi relies on loyalty offers but lacks deeper cross-sell automation and recipe-driven shopping
Fast delivery is priced separately and excluded from subscription and free-threshold rules, creating complexity; a simpler slot/price model would improve conversion
Limited seller/marketplace model — Rimi operates supply-controlled assortment (good for quality) but leaves a gap for curated local/third‑party marketplaces within the checkout

pricingPlans.name: Delivery subscription (60 days)
pricingPlans.note: Waives delivery fee for qualifying orders during subscription period (does not apply to fast deliveries)

pricingPlans.name: Standard delivery
pricingPlans.note: Standard same‑day delivery fees; free if order > €49.98 (Latvia)

pricingPlans.name: Fast delivery / express
pricingPlans.note: Express/partner deliveries (58–90 minutes) are charged separately and excluded from some free/ subscription rules

pricingEvidence.label: Piegādes un Saņemšanas veidi (delivery page)
pricingEvidence.claim: Shows delivery fees (€1.99–€3.99 standard), free delivery over €49.98 in Latvia, fast pickup/delivery options and 60‑day delivery subscription pricing

pricingEvidence.label: Delivery subscription product page
pricingEvidence.claim: Delivery subscription (60 days) sold as a product in the e‑store (en product listing)

companyEvidence.label: Rimi Baltic (corporate site)
companyEvidence.claim: Rimi Baltic operates the Rimi retail chains in Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania and launched e‑store solution (omnichannel retail)

companyEvidence.label: Rimi Baltic — Wikipedia
companyEvidence.claim: Background on Rimi Baltic, store counts and corporate history (operations in Baltics since the late 1990s; Rimi Baltic formed 2004/2005)

reviewEvidence.label: Reddit — r/latvia thread
reviewEvidence.claim: User reports of recurring out-of-stock items and substitution issues when ordering from Rimi e-veikals

reviewEvidence.label: Sūdzības.lv (customer complaints)
reviewEvidence.claim: Public customer complaints about product quality, service and store issues (site collecting consumer complaints)

featureEvidence.label: Help — delivery rules and order changes
featureEvidence.claim: Documents ability to change/augment orders until picking starts, delivery area notes, storage temperature rules for frozen goods

featureEvidence.label: Rimi e-veikals homepage
featureEvidence.claim: Product page promoting same‑day delivery, Drive pickup and mobile app shopping

featureEvidence.label: Google Play — Rimi app
featureEvidence.claim: Mobile app offers shopping lists, personal offers and functionality linked to the e‑store (Mans Rimi loyalty integration)

strengths: Strong omnichannel retail footprint and brand trust across the Baltics (many physical stores provide local fulfillment and cold‑chain capability)
Multiple fulfilment options (home delivery, Rimi Drive, in‑store pickup) including sub‑hour fast pickup/delivery in selected zones
Competitive and simple delivery subscription product that reduces per‑order friction for frequent shoppers; free delivery threshold incentivizes larger baskets
Integrated loyalty (Mans Rimi) and mobile app for personalised offers and seamless in‑store/e‑commerce experience
weaknesses: Delivery coverage and slot availability are inconsistent outside main cities — rural customers may have limited or infrequent service
Inventory transparency and out‑of‑stock handling frustrate customers (many report learning of missing items only after picking), increasing operational churn and returns
Express/partner delivery is excluded from subscription/free‑threshold rules, adding price complexity and undermining subscription value for urgent orders
Limited developer/API presence or marketplace capability — hard for partners or B2B customers to integrate or resell through Rimi’s platform
